title: dicentra formosa subsp. nevadensis
intro: Dicentra formosa subsp. nevadensis, also known as Nevada bleeding heart, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is known for its heart-shaped flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues, including fever reduction and wound healing. Dicentra formosa subsp. nevadensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues.
